scope: Tier-2 auth-hook migration, Pass B reviewed: the 4 legacy-parity read streams only (products, plans, customers, subscriptions), each a distinct GraphQL root QUERY field on the SAME single POST /graphql endpoint. Pass B full-surface research was ATTEMPTED against Stigg's real live schema (see docs.md 'Pass B full-surface expansion research findings') but blocked: docs.stigg.io's GraphQL reference and schema-export pages are client-side-rendered SPAs with no crawlable static content, live introspection against api.stigg.io/graphql requires an authenticated API key this research pass did not have, and the npm registry (a secondary source for generated SDK types) was unreachable from this environment. Every covered/excluded entry below is declared method GET (not the literal wire HTTP verb, which is always POST for GraphQL) to correctly signal READ semantics to connectorgen validate's capabilities.write/read cross-check (design SS E.1 rule 4) -- Stigg's GraphQL transport uses POST for both queries and mutations, so the wire verb alone cannot distinguish them.

endpoints.path: /graphql (mutations: provisionCustomer, reportUsage, coupon/subscription mutations, and others)

excluded.reason: mutation root fields and their exact argument/input-type shapes could not be confirmed this pass: GraphQL introspection against the live endpoint requires an authenticated API key (not available to this research pass) and docs.stigg.io's API reference is a client-side-rendered SPA with no crawlable static content; a fabricated mutation name/argument shape would hard-error against the real API rather than degrade gracefully, so no write action was added. stigg remains read-only (capabilities.write: false); see docs.md's Pass B research findings section

web-search-derived secondary evidence indicates this is a real Relay-style paginated connection (filter/sorting/paging args, edges{node{...CouponFragment}} response shape) rather than the bare-array shape the 4 covered streams use, but this could not be confirmed against a primary source (introspection blocked, docs SPA uncrawlable); implementing a stream against an unconfirmed connection shape risks a hard runtime error against the live API. See docs.md's Pass B research findings section

exact root field name (addOns vs addons) and response shape (bare array vs Relay connection) could not be confirmed against a primary source this pass (introspection blocked, docs SPA uncrawlable); see docs.md's Pass B research findings section

exact root field name(s) and response shape could not be confirmed against a primary source this pass (introspection blocked, docs SPA uncrawlable); web-search evidence only names a PromotionalEntitlementFragment field set, not a confirmed top-level query root field. See docs.md's Pass B research findings section

existence and exact shape of an invoices root query field could not be confirmed against any source this pass (not mentioned in the secondary web-search evidence that did confirm coupons/subscriptions/customers); carried forward from the pre-Pass-B placeholder as an unverified candidate, not a confirmed gap
